competitor_patterns: Word count dominance: Bikerumor ranks #1 for 'road cycling glasses review' with 24,888 words — exhaustive multi-product roundups with hands-on test notes outrank shorter brand-centric posts; Active ranks #1 at 5,539 words. Our longest posts top out at ~4,639 words.

Neutral editorial voice: Top-ranking content (Active, Bikerumor) frames itself as independent journalism ('tested', 'ranked', 'top picks') rather than brand advocacy — they include competitor products and only then highlight a winner, which builds credibility Google rewards.

Structured product taxonomy in H2s: Bikerumor uses individual product names as H2 headers (Julbo Density, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour) creating standalone indexable sections per product — this captures long-tail '[brand] cycling glasses review 2026' queries as sub-rankings.

FAQ schema near-universal at top: Active.com includes FAQ schema; all top-3 competitors appear to use structured data. Our FAQ schema coverage is only 33% across published posts, leaving rich-result slots unclaimed.

Interchangeable-lens category owned by Evil Eye: evileye.com holds position #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a dedicated category/collection page — not a blog post — suggesting commercial/product pages can outrank editorial for transactional lens-swap queries.
our_gaps: Thin short-form posts dilute authority: 22 of our 40+ posts are under 1,400 words. Google's top results for cycling glasses queries average 5,000–25,000 words. These thin posts likely split topical authority without ranking individually, creating cannibalization risk on core terms like 'best cycling glasses 2026'.

Missing independent multi-brand test framing: Our posts lead with Velluto advantages and treat competitors as foils. Top competitors include 5–8 products with genuine pros/cons per brand before naming a winner — our comparative posts lack this balanced structure that earns editorial trust signals.

No standalone interchangeable-lens product/category page: Evil Eye ranks #1 with a shop category page, not a blog post. We have blog content on interchangeable lenses but no dedicated SEO-optimised product landing page targeting 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' as a commercial query.

FAQ schema on only 33% of posts: 27+ posts are missing FAQ schema, including high-impression pages targeting 'oakley alternatives' and 'best cycling glasses' clusters — these are confirmed near-top positions where FAQ rich results would directly boost CTR without requiring rank improvement.

No content targeting specific model-level queries: We have no posts structured around '[competitor model] vs Velluto StradaPro' at the H2/section level (e.g. 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs StradaPro', 'POC Devour vs StradaPro') — Bikerumor captures these long-tail clicks by naming models in headers.
keyword_opportunities: oakley sutro lite alternative 2026 — Bikerumor ranks #1 featuring Oakley Sutro Lite; this model-specific alternative query has clear commercial intent and we have zero dedicated coverage at model level.

poc devour cycling glasses review — POC Devour appears in Bikerumor's top-ranking H2s; a 'POC Devour vs budget alternative' angle targeting this query captures comparison shoppers and links naturally to StradaPro.

interchangeable lens cycling glasses 2026 best value — Evil Eye owns the head term; a long-tail value angle ('best value interchangeable lens cycling glasses') is less contested and aligns directly with StradaPro's click-in lens system at a lower price point.

cycling glasses 25g lightweight 2026 — no competitor appears to target weight-specific queries around 25g; this ultra-specific query would be near-zero competition and directly showcases StradaPro's primary hardware differentiator.

cycling glasses 30 day trial risk free — zero-competition branded-feature query; as AI Overview and Perplexity increasingly surface purchase-reassurance content, owning 'risk free cycling glasses trial' positions Velluto as the only brand with this offer in organic results.
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 5,500 words for any 'best cycling glasses/sunglasses' roundup post — match Active.com's floor and approach Bikerumor's depth; include at least 6 products with individual H2 sections per product (model name in H2), genuine pros/cons per product, then name StradaPro as the value winner in a summary section.

Add FAQ schema to every post published from today forward — with the current 33% coverage rate, every new post without FAQ schema is a missed rich-result opportunity; include minimum 4 FAQs per post targeting question-format long-tail variants of the primary keyword (e.g. 'Are interchangeable lens cycling glasses worth it?', 'What is the lightest cycling glass in 2026?').

Use specific competitor model names in H2 headers within comparison posts (e.g. 'Oakley Sutro Lite', 'POC Devour', 'Julbo Density') — this captures model-level long-tail queries as sub-rankings within a single page, replicating Bikerumor's structure that earns position #1.

Lead every post with an independent editorial frame ('We tested X pairs over Y km') before introducing Velluto — top competitors earn trust by appearing non-commercial first; position StradaPro as the conclusion of evidence, not the premise, to reduce bounce from readers who distrust brand sites.

Consolidate thin posts (under 1,400 words) into the nearest thematic parent: the 8 anti-fog posts, 4 interchangeable-lens posts, and 3 Oakley-alternatives posts each should be merged or internally linked into one 5,000+ word authoritative hub — do not publish new thin posts on already-covered sub-topics.

For all Oakley-alternatives content, hardcode the 25g weight comparison in the first 150 words and in the comparison table — GSC shows 481 combined impressions across Oakley-alternatives queries at positions 4–8; the weight differentiator is the most concrete, scannable reason to switch that competitors cannot copy.

Include a 'Who should NOT buy this' section in every product-focused post — this pattern appears in high-trust editorial reviews and signals genuine assessment to both readers and Google's quality raters, improving E-E-A-T signals on brand-operated content.
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to all 27+ posts currently missing it — prioritise by impression volume: start with 'best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked' (243 impressions, pos 6.7), 'gravel-vs-road-cycling-glasses-2026', and all short-form posts under 1,400 words that have existing FAQ sections but no markup. This alone could recover 2–4 CTR percentage points on near-top queries.

Add a comparison table to all posts currently missing one — 19 posts lack tables; for posts targeting 'best [X] cycling glasses' queries, a scannable spec table (weight, lens system, price, UV rating, trial policy) is a confirmed pattern in every top-3 result and increases time-on-page; prioritise the 5 posts with over 1,100 words that already have comparison content in prose form.

Fix internal linking to consolidate Oakley-alternatives authority — there are at least 5 separate Oakley-alternatives posts (best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked, best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-lighter-cheaper, best-oakley-alternative-cycling-sunglasses-2026-velluto, oakley-alternative-road-cycling-glasses-2026-guide, best-alternatives-to-oakley-cycling-glasses-in-2026); designate one as the canonical hub, canonicalise or 301-redirect the thinnest variants, and funnel all internal links to the hub — this clusters ranking signals onto one URL instead of splitting across five.
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Shop | Why: The query is a misspelling of the brand name (250 impressions, CTR 0.8%) suggesting branded searchers who mistype; a clear brand-affirmation title with 'Official Shop' reassures navigational intent and lifts CTR from 0.8% — also add 'veluto' as an alt spelling in the page's meta keywords or schema name field.

oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026 — Lighter & Cheaper | Why: Current CTR is 2.9% at position 6.7 — adding '2026', 'Lighter' (25g differentiator) and 'Cheaper' directly addresses the comparison searcher's core motivation in under 60 characters and matches the emotional trigger that drives clicks on alternatives queries.

oakley sunglasses alternative → New title: Oakley Sunglasses Alternative: 25g, Half the Price | Why: CTR 1.5% at position 6.7 — specificity with '25g' and a price-comparison hook differentiates this result from generic 'alternatives' listicles; the colon structure also improves scannability in mobile SERPs.

best oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 | Tested | Why: CTR 1.6% at position 8.1 — adding 'for Cyclists' narrows intent match (reducing irrelevant impressions), '2026' signals freshness, and '| Tested' mirrors the editorial credibility signal used by Bikerumor and Active who rank above us.

oakley alternative → New title: Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses — StradaPro 2026 | Why: CTR 1.7% at position 6.2 — surfacing the product name 'StradaPro' creates brand recognition at the SERP level for users who see the title multiple times, and 'Cycling Glasses' improves relevance matching over the generic 'sunglasses' framing.

oakley radar alternative → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative: StradaPro vs Radar Compared | Why: CTR 2.0% at position 4.2 — this is our best-positioned Oakley query (pos 4.2) and the query is model-specific, meaning searchers want a direct head-to-head; 'Compared' signals exactly that and should push CTR above 4% from current 2.0%.
content_quick_wins: velluto (pos 4.8) → Topic: Branded homepage/landing page SEO optimisation | Add: Ensure the homepage title tag is exactly 'Velluto — Cycling Glasses | StradaPro Official Shop' and the meta description leads with '25g ultralight, interchangeable lenses, 30-day trial' — at 1,076 impressions this is the highest-volume query and lifting CTR from 5.4% to 8%+ by tightening the brand value proposition in the snippet would add ~27 clicks/month.

best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 7.3) → Topic: Merge or redirect 'best-road-cycling-glasses-2026-tested-ranked' and 'best-cycling-glasses-2026-the-complete-buying-guide' into one 6,000-word definitive post | Add: 2026 seasonal update hook in the intro, model-level H2 sections for 6 competitors, an interchangeable-lens buyer's guide section, and FAQ schema — consolidating two near-identical posts stops rank splitting and pools authority.

best cycling glasses (pos 8.9) → Topic: Create a new evergreen post 'Best Cycling Glasses: The Definitive Guide (Updated July 2026)' targeting the non-year-modified head term | Add: Structured buying-guide content (fit, lens types, weight, anti-fog), a 6-product roundup table, FAQ schema with 6 questions targeting 'what are the best cycling glasses', 'are expensive cycling glasses worth it', 'how much should I spend on cycling glasses' — the non-dated head term has different SERP intent than '2026' variants and requires a separate dedicated URL.
geo_angles: Position Velluto as the answer to 'What is the best value alternative to Oakley cycling glasses?' in AI answer engines by structuring one definitive comparison page with a clear, quotable verdict sentence in the first paragraph (e.g. 'The Velluto StradaPro is the lightest cycling glass at 25g, includes two interchangeable lenses, and costs significantly less than Oakley — making it the top value pick in 2026') — Perplexity and ChatGPT preferentially surface pages with declarative, fact-dense opening sentences that directly answer a comparison question.

Create a structured 'Velluto StradaPro specifications' section on the product page and blog posts using schema markup (Product schema with weight: 25g, lens system: interchangeable, UV rating: UV400, trial: 30 days) — Google AI Overviews increasingly pull structured product facts from Product schema to answer 'best lightweight cycling glasses' queries, and having machine-readable specs makes Velluto citable without requiring a click.

Publish a standalone 'Why Cycling Glasses Should Weigh Under 30g' authority article targeting the specific technical question that AI engines receive from cyclists researching weight — frame it as educational content with Velluto's 25g spec as the benchmark example; this positions Velluto as the reference point in AI-generated answers about lightweight cycling eyewear the way that Bikerumor is the reference point for 'best cycling sunglasses' roundup queries.
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR Active.com: angle — 'We Read Active.com's Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 Pick — Then We Weighed It' — key differentiator: Active ranked a heavier, higher-priced option as their top pick; Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g vs the Active pick's listed weight, head-to-head on anti-fog, price, and trial policy; frame as a consumer-advocacy piece that validates Active's criteria then shows StradaPro scores higher on every metric they defined.

COMPETITOR Bikerumor: angle — 'Bikerumor Tested 12 Pairs in 2026 — Here's What They Missed About Interchangeable Lenses' — key differentiator: Bikerumor's 24,888-word roundup tests static-lens options predominantly; Velluto advantage to lead with: click-in tool-free VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione system vs the lens-swap complexity of Oakley Sutro Lite and POC Devour; angle positions Velluto as filling the gap in Bikerumor's own test criteria.

COMPETITOR Evil Eye (evileye.com): angle — 'Evil Eye Interchangeable Lenses vs Velluto StradaPro: Same System, 40% Less Money' — key differentiator: Evil Eye holds position #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a premium price point; Velluto advantage to lead with: direct lens-system feature parity comparison (click-in mechanism, UV400, anti-fog) with price transparency and 30-day trial that Evil Eye does not offer; include a side-by-side spec table as the centrepiece.
